phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-farm from range and concede the duel question entirely — your level 1 is a wallet, not a weapon.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the gold card re-prices his every step-up. Lock yellow when he walks forward and the lane behaves.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: stacked-deck autos plus card cycling is a real short trade — but your game is the clock, not the lane.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — Q the wave, lock cards on cooldown, and watch side lanes like they're your KDA.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Destiny online — THE level in this lane. Every shove is now a bot-lane death sentence with a 130-second timer.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your gold-card tempo picks in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late TF is a coin with two good sides: split the side lane R-safe, or hold Destiny as the pick-and-collapse button.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your gold-card tempo picks beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Your gold-card tempo picks out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at level 2 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 your gold-card tempo picks beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your gold-card tempo picks out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — gold-card tempo picks edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Lich Bane spike: gold card chunks half bars. The lane still isn't the point — the map pays your salary.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late TF is a coin with two good sides: split the side lane R-safe, or hold Destiny as the pick-and-collapse button.

phases.why: Late TF is a coin with two good sides: split the side lane R-safe, or hold Destiny as the pick-and-collapse button.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-farm from range and concede the duel question entirely — your level 1 is a wallet, not a weapon.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the gold card re-prices his every step-up. Lock yellow when he walks forward and the lane behaves.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: stacked-deck autos plus card cycling is a real short trade — but your game is the clock, not the lane.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — Q the wave, lock cards on cooldown, and watch side lanes like they're your KDA.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Destiny online — THE level in this lane. Every shove is now a bot-lane death sentence with a 130-second timer.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Lich Bane spike: gold card chunks half bars. The lane still isn't the point — the map pays your salary.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late TF is a coin with two good sides: split the side lane R-safe, or hold Destiny as the pick-and-collapse button.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your gold-card tempo picks beat Veigar's cage-stack inevitability on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — gold-card tempo picks edge out the cage-stack inevitability when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your gold-card tempo picks beat Veigar's cage-stack inevitability on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — gold-card tempo picks edge out the cage-stack inevitability when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Destiny online — THE level in this lane. Every shove is now a bot-lane death sentence with a 130-second timer.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Your gold-card tempo picks out-muscle Kassadin's void-shield scaling at level 1 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 your gold-card tempo picks beat the void-shield scaling in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Kassadin answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your gold-card tempo picks out-muscle Kassadin's void-shield scaling at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — Q the wave, lock cards on cooldown, and watch side lanes like they're your KDA.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your gold-card tempo picks beat Kassadin's void-shield scaling on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.
